mechanism_narrative: High real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. The relationship is persistent and does not require a special activation condition.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p < 0.10) over any rolling 5-year window, or if the mean forward return during above-median RSAFS periods is not greater than zero at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, monetary policy is extremely accommodative, lowering discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. This threshold is meaningful because it marks a binding zero lower bound regime.
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return in months when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not significantly greater than zero (one-tailed t-test, p > 0.05) or if the win rate (fraction of positive returns) is below 55% over any 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate (UNRATE above 7%) signals labor market slack and weak aggregate demand, which depresses corporate earnings and investor confidence, resulting in negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return during periods when UNRATE > 7% is not negative or if the proportion of negative 5-day returns is less than 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over a three-month period indicate a synchronous contraction in supply and labor markets, presaging a recession. This joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO decreases over 3 months AND PAYEMS decreases over 3 months
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following concurrent 3-month declines in both series is not negative, or if the joint signal does not reduce false positives compared to each series individually (assessed via confusion matrix over a 10-year period).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate (i.e., negative real interest rates), monetary policy is effectively accommodative beyond the nominal stance. This depresses risk-free real yields, pushing investors into equities and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return during negative real rate months is not significantly higher than during positive real rate months (two-sample t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window, or if the average excess return relative to positive real rate months is less than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), rising housing starts (HOUST) signal robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a month. The effect is contingent on the monetary regime; when rates are higher, the same housing data may be interpreted negatively as a harbinger of tightening.
failure_condition: If the Spearman correlation between HOUST and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p < 0.10) when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or if the correlation is not stronger than when FEDFUNDS >= 1% (i.e., no regime modulation).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.3% indicates expanding manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which signals economic strength and supports corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following months with INDPRO growth > 0.3% is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window, or if the hit rate (percentage of positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail sales (RSAFS above 400 billion) and low unemployment (UNRATE below 6%) represent a Goldilocks macro environment with strong consumption and ample labor supply spare. This joint signal is a powerful predictor of positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a five-day horizon due to immediate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return when both conditions are true is not positive with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or if the joint signal does not exceed the predictive power of each series individually (assessed via nested model likelihood ratio test).
